source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
INTRODUCTION

We value candidates who have significant experience with managing complexity, working independently, and are motivated by hard problems.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The ability to have real impact on mission-critical workflows

 - The chance to use state-of-the-art tech to solve novel problems with mission partners

 - The opportunity to define your role through time as we grow

 - Competitive salary and equity upside with annual merit refreshes based on impact

Position Overview

As an early full-stack engineer, you’ll help build Conductor’s core application: a user-facing web platform for large scale document search and review. You’ll work on document-processing pipelines, an agentic workflow execution system, database scaling problems, and generally pushing SOTA LLMs to their limits. Our tech stack is TypeScript/React, Python/FastAPI, and Postgres.



This is a product engineering role as much as a systems role. You’ll design and craft interfaces to make complex document-centric workflows intuitive and joyful to interact with. You’ll partner closely with users in defense, government, and regulated commercial environments to empower them to move faster without sacrificing accuracy or security.



We are seeking an engineer with a generalist skillset and attitude, diverse technical background, and a willingness and ability to learn new technologies on the job. You'll be working on a small, tight-knit technical team, with the opportunity to help define and mature our architecture and processes as we grow. You'll be expected to communicate complex technical concepts with all members of the team and lead product efforts involving multiple team members with varying levels of technical literacy.



Location This role is open to fully remote candidates within the US. East Coast and especially NYC/DC preferred.



Key Responsibilities

 - Optimize search and review agents for accuracy and consistency

 - Build polished, reliable user-facing features in a Next.js/React application

 - Design backend APIs, data models, and workflow execution paths that support responsive frontend experiences

 - Write high quality code within a small team that cares about implementation quality, maintainability, and performance

 - Help design and improve the architecture of the system

 - Work with users and other team members to help define and refine product direction

 - Contribute to company strategy, nurture and shape our growing culture, and take on diverse responsibilities as we evolve.

Qualifications

 - US citizenship required; this role supports work subject to US government contract obligations and export control regulations (ITAR/EAR)

 - 3+ years as a professional software engineer

 - Strong communication skills, both written and verbal

 - Strong product sense and care for usable interfaces

 - Proactive mindset, with a knack for identifying opportunities and taking initiative and ownership

 - Experience with system design

 - Proficiency in Python and TypeScript/React

 - Experience with LLMs and AI engineering techniques

Nice to Haves

 - Experience in defense, intelligence, national security, or other regulated operational domains

 - Experience interacting with end-users

 - Familiarity with document processing pipelines

 - Experience at an early-stage startup

 - Active or current security clearance

The base salary range for this role is $160,000–$250,000, with the opportunity for significant equity commensurate with a Series A stage opportunity. Starting pay will be based on a number of factors including qualifications, experience, and location. This range reflects base salary only and does not include equity or other compensation components.

In accordance with New York pay transparency law, the base salary range listed above applies to candidates in New York state. Compensation for candidates in other locations may vary based on local market conditions and will fall within the range stated above.

source_jobs.title: Dynamic Duo
source_jobs.description:
INTRODUCTION

We value candidates who have significant experience with managing complexity, working independently, and are motivated by hard problems.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The ability to have real impact on mission-critical workflows

 - The chance to use state-of-the-art tech to solve novel problems with mission partners

 - The opportunity to define your role through time as we grow

 - Competitive salary and equity upside with annual merit refreshes based on impact

Dynamic Duo

Peanut Butter and Jelly, Serena and Venus, Billie and Finneas, Batman and Robin, Pam and Jim, Ja Rule and Ashanti, Buffett and Munger, Lennon and McCartney...

You love working together, why not start on your next adventure together?

This role is for a combined submission of any two roles we have open, such as an Engineer plus Customer Strategist who've built incredible things for customers together, an Operations Strategist plus a rock-star Recruiter who can scale a business faster than either one independently, or even two Engineers who, when paired together, make amazing things.

We'll interview you at the same pace, including some interviews together, and give you each an offer at the same time. You're also welcome to apply to the roles independently.

What We're Looking For

 - Expertise building and scaling software capabilities for end-customers together

 - Fast iteration cycles, high-impact, and strong communication skills

 - Ability to work as a team and also independently with other customer-facing roles

 - Individuals who are independently in the top 10% of their function and, when your powers are combined, in the top 1% of impact at your current company

 - An active DoD Secret clearance or higher is required at time of hire for customer-facing roles (Customer Strategist and Full Stack Engineer); this requirement is mandated by the classified nature of work performed under US government contracts and is non-negotiable

 - US citizenship required for all roles by applicable federal law and government contract obligations (ITAR/EAR); we are unable to consider green card holders or sponsor visas

Steps to Apply

 1. Read the descriptions of the other roles to ensure you both qualify; as part of the interview process, we'll interview you separately and together

 2. Use AI to combine your resumes and be sure to include 1) specific outcomes you've accomplished together, 2) your individual strengths and weaknesses and how that makes you a dynamic pair

 3. Apply via the form on the role

Compensation Each candidate will receive an individual offer reflective of their role, qualifications, experience, and clearance level. Salary ranges for each role are listed in the respective job descriptions. All offers are made independently and simultaneously.

ConductorAI is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. Reasonable accommodations are available for qualified individuals with disabilities throughout the application and hiring process.

source_jobs.title: Operations Strategist (Cleared)
source_jobs.description:
INTRODUCTION

We value candidates who have significant experience with managing complexity, working independently, and are motivated by hard problems.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The ability to have real impact on mission-critical workflows

 - The chance to use state-of-the-art tech to solve novel problems with mission partners

 - The opportunity to define your role through time as we grow

 - Competitive salary and equity upside with annual merit refreshes based on impact

About the Role You'll operate at the intersection of company operations and program management. You are the first responder when things go wrong, a process architect when things are going right, and a steady hand and thought partner throughout.



Location This role is open to candidates based in New York, NY.



Qualifications

 - Active DoD Secret clearance or higher required at time of hire; this requirement is mandated by the classified nature of work performed under US government contracts and is non-negotiable

 - US citizenship required by applicable federal law and government contract obligations (ITAR/EAR); we are unable to consider green card holders or sponsor visas for this role

 - 2–5 years working in fast-paced startup environments, specifically venture-backed companies and government-adjacent work

 - Experience working with DoD partners, USG, and/or IC components

 - Excellent communicator, highly adept at building lightweight processes, and demonstrated ability to adapt quickly in the face of new information or needs

 - Numerous examples of how your specific contributions have led to meaningful growth, including but not limited to new revenue, accelerated hiring, process reduction, and/or fostering company culture

 - Skilled at the use of modern technology, including LLMs and AI-enabled workflows, project management software (Linear, Notion, Google Workspace, etc.), and are a quick study

 - Demonstrated expertise in building relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including engineering, product, and customer team members

 - Exceptional references

Desired Qualifications

 - Active TS or TS/SCI clearance

 - Experience building or managing industrial security programs, FSO responsibilities, or government facility access

 - Experience working in or supporting classified environments

What You'll Do

 - Own multiple concurrent workstreams in partnership with the Founders and company team members, including planning, operational execution, and stakeholder communications, using lightweight frameworks that enable rapid iteration without heavy overhead

 - Maintain a broad, accurate state across all projects you touch; proactively identify risks and surface blockers, and drive prioritization decisions in close partnership with internal, customer, and technical partners

 - Act as surge support when operational demands spike, stepping in wherever the team needs the most support

Company Operations

 - Build the industrial security function, including employee access to government systems and facilities, clearances, etc.

 - Work closely with company leadership to identify specific challenges related to internal company operations, including hiring, communication, program management, tool use, and security, and develop plans to address these challenges to help the company scale

 - Partner with the COO to maintain company spaces, manage vendors, plan off-sites, develop company policies, and foster a company culture of collaboration and positivity

 - Build and continuously refine scalable processes for onboarding, security, training, and required reporting — enabling the business to grow without sacrificing compliance posture or speed

 - Craft and implement process improvements that reduce friction and free up teammates to focus on their highest-value work

 - Partner with internal, legal, and government teams on security-related contract preparation, submissions, and compliance tracking

 - Identify resources and experts, internal

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
INTRODUCTION

We value candidates who have significant experience with managing complexity, working independently, and are motivated by hard problems.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The ability to have real impact on mission-critical workflows

 - The chance to use state-of-the-art tech to solve novel problems with mission partners

 - The opportunity to define your role through time as we grow

 - Competitive salary and equity upside with annual merit refreshes based on impact

Position Overview

As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you will sit at the intersection of engineering and customer partnership. You will be onsite with customers regularly, building trust, diagnosing needs, and translating user feedback directly into product improvements. You are still fundamentally an engineer: you will write code, debug systems, and help shape the architecture of a platform built to leverage LLMs and modern AI application techniques. But you are equally comfortable in a customer briefing room as you are in a terminal.

This role is for someone who finds it energizing to own the full loop: understand the problem, build the solution, deploy it, and watch it create value for users in real time. You should be comfortable with ambiguity, have a bias for action, and take pride in the quality and reliability of what you ship. You will work on a small, tight-knit team and will be expected to communicate complex technical concepts clearly to users and stakeholders with varying levels of technical fluency.

As an early team member, you will also help shape our culture, processes, and product direction. We expect you to measure your work by impact, not activity.

Location

Washington, DC area. Up to 50% travel domestically and OCONUS.

Key Responsibilities

 - Go onsite to customer environments to configure, optimize, and support ConductorAI software, including customizing workflows for specific customer use cases and troubleshooting the system

 - Write and maintain production-quality code in Python and TypeScript within a small engineering team

 - Work directly with users to gather feedback, understand workflows, and translate insights into actionable product requirements

 - Debug and improve system reliability, performance, and usability based on real-world customer usage

 - Build and deliver technical demonstrations to new and existing customers

 - Collaborate with the product and engineering team to develop new features, with a direct line from customer need to shipped capability

 - Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and improve operational efficiency at customer sites

 - As a core team member, contribute to company strategy, culture, and evolving responsibilities as we grow

Qualifications

 - Active DoD Secret clearance or higher required at time of hire; required due to the classified nature of work performed under US government contracts

 - US citizenship required by applicable federal law and government contract obligations (ITAR/EAR); we are unable to consider green card holders or sponsor visas for this role

 - 2+ years of professional software engineering or forward deployed engineering experience

 - Proficiency in Python and TypeScript

 - Experience working directly with end users or customers in a technical capacity

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences

 - Comfort operating with high autonomy in ambiguous, fast-moving environments

 - Experience with LLMs and modern AI application techniques

 - Ability to travel up to 40% domestically and OCONUS

Desired Qualifications

 - Active TS or TS/SCI clearance

 - Experience in defense, intelligence, or national security environments

 - Experience at an early-stage startup or in fast-moving, ambiguous environments

 - Background in system design or full-stack development

 - Existing relationships with DoD or civil agency stakeholders

Compensation

The base salary range for thi

source_jobs.title: Product Design Lead
source_jobs.description:
INTRODUCTION

We value candidates who have significant experience with managing complexity, working independently, and are motivated by hard problems.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The ability to have real impact on mission-critical workflows

 - The chance to use state-of-the-art tech to solve novel problems with mission partners

 - The opportunity to define your role through time as we grow

 - Competitive salary and equity upside with annual merit refreshes based on impact

What You'll Do

 - Build an AI-Native Design System: Build a scalable, React-based component-driven design system that engineers can and will use by default.

 - Ensure the design system is directly consumable by AI tools (like Claude Code and Codex) rather than being siloed in Figma.

 - Empower Engineering: Proactively educate the engineering team by showing them how to leverage the design system to make good, independent UI/UX choices.

 - Introduce and lead rapid prototyping techniques to quickly gather feedback on early feature ideas before heavy engineering begins.

 - Help review meaningful frontend changes for design concerns.

 - Own the Design Process: Establish a new, scalable design process that matches our fast pace and takes design out of the bottleneck.

 - Position product design as a proactive product owner that iterates on the platform without requiring direct hand-holding.

 - Establish tight feedback loops with customer strategists and end-users to inform product direction and usability improvements.
   
   

Qualifications

 - 3–5+ years of experience designing complex digital products, with a portfolio showing real shipped work, clear problem framing, and strong visual craft

 - Design & Technical Hybrid: Strong grasp of user experience principles and frontend skills in React; comfortable opening a PR, tweaking TSX/CSS, or collaborating at a technical level with engineers

 - Interaction Design & IA Skills: Ability to untangle dense, stateful workflows and make them feel obvious and intuitive

 - Visual & Systems Design Instincts: Keen eye for typography, spacing, hierarchy, and motion that make a product feel cohesive

 - Cross-Functional Partner: Operates as a true partner to engineering and product, not a downstream service

 - Bias for Action & Autonomy: Spots design debt and fixes it systematically without waiting for permission; knows how to unblock others by creating scalable systems

 - Design Tooling: Proficiency in Figma with comfort moving seamlessly between rapid exploration and high-fidelity, polished work

 - Clear Communicator: Able to articulate design decisions clearly across audiences including customers, engineers, and operational stakeholders

 

Desired Qualifications

 - AI Tool Fluency: Experience with AI-enabled prototyping and using tools like Claude to multiply output (e.g. translating code to canvas or setting up AI-friendly UI components)

 - Complex Domains: Experience designing tools for logistics, operations, defense, healthcare, or other dense operational sectors

 - Startup DNA: Experience working in fast-paced environments with evolving requirements and ambitious, mission-driven goals



Location This role is open to candidates based in New York, NY.

 

The base salary range for this role is $180,000-220,000, with the opportunity for significant equity commensurate with a Series A stage opportunity. Starting pay will be based on a number of factors including qualifications, experience, and location. This range reflects base salary only and does not include equity or other compensation components.

 

In accordance with the New York City Pay Transparency Law, the base salary range listed above reflects the compensation for this position for candidates in New York City. This range was established in good faith at the time of posting and represents the full band for this role.

 

ConductorAI is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin,

source_jobs.title: Customer Strategist (Cleared)
source_jobs.description:
INTRODUCTION

We value candidates who have significant experience with managing complexity, working independently, and are motivated by hard problems.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The ability to have real impact on mission-critical workflows

 - The chance to use state-of-the-art tech to solve novel problems with mission partners

 - The opportunity to define your role through time as we grow

 - Competitive salary and equity upside with annual merit refreshes based on impact

Position Overview This multi-faceted role involves a combination of strategy, sales, and relationship management, requiring a highly organized and self-motivated individual. You'll be on the forefront of building and deploying our software to mission users, working closely with engineers to develop new features and capabilities, and scaling the team over time. You need to have sharp user and product acumen, experience with intense customers (mission environments or critical industry companies), be adept at learning about new problems and technologies, and have a nose for valuable use cases. You should be a strategic thinker who can identify new opportunities, build strong client and partner relationships, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to achieve business objectives.

You will play a large role in defining the product direction and tone of our platform — as such, you should want to measure your work based on impact, not pats on the back, and you need to have a maniacal focus on delivering value to users. You will be responsible for determining how we work with customers and, in turn, help steer the strategy of our company.

Finally, you need to be comfortable working on a tight-knit team, including a history of highly productive relationships with engineers and a willingness and ability to take ownership of hard problems. As we scale, you should also be comfortable defining processes to work smarter, not harder, and to support additional team members.



Location This role is based in the Washington, DC area.



Key Responsibilities

 - Go onsite and work closely with customers to execute on various products, including scoping, product feedback sessions, trainings, data analysis, key presentations, and user support

 - Build and deliver demos to new customers

 - Drive increased revenue with customers through shaping new opportunities for expansion via feature sets, use cases, or value creation

 - Develop and grow relationships with executive-level stakeholders at our customer organizations and conferences

 - Embody the voice of the user to drive our product roadmap and ensure engineers have access to key customer insights

 - Identify opportunities to scale customer and contract operations as we grow, including project management, planning, and forecasting

 - As a core member of our team, contribute to company strategy, nurture and shape our budding culture, and take on diverse responsibilities as we evolve

Qualifications

 - Active DoD Secret clearance or higher required at time of hire; this requirement is mandated by the classified nature of work performed under US government contracts and is non-negotiable

 - US citizenship required by applicable federal law and government contract obligations (ITAR/EAR); we are unable to consider green card holders or sponsor visas for this role

 - 2–5 years of experience embedding in technical customer workflows, understanding customer data, and driving new product features

 - A bias for action and distinct aptitude for problem solving in ambiguous environments

 - Technical fluency, competency, and curiosity

 - Ability to travel up to 50% domestically and OCONUS

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

Desired Qualifications

 - Active TS or TS/SCI clearance

 - Experience working in defense, intelligence, or national security environments

 - Experience at an early-stage startup or in fast-moving, ambiguous environments

 - Existing relationships with DoD or civil agency stakeholders

Th

notes: High-tech startup with strong engineering focus, some cleared roles, and a mix of product and customer-facing positions.
